description: Lock the 2026-06-25 source-check of merchant_partnership_contracts to Old Assyrian/Kultepe written merchant partnership contracts.

checks.reason: The corrected node is anchored to the Old Assyrian period and 19th-century BCE Assur-Kanes/Kultepe merchant evidence.
The scoped technology is written cuneiform merchant contracts, so durable commercial recordkeeping is required.
Ancient law-code traditions are shared legal context, not a direct claim that one code created Old Assyrian partnership contracts.
Silver, tin, textiles, prices, and exchange values require metrology for scaling and settlement before coined currency.
The corrected scope must not route through the broad later Classical contract_law node.
The corrected Bronze Age scope predates coined currency and uses metrology/silver valuation instead.
Private merchant tablets and written commercial records are not the same as public archive infrastructure.
Classical naval shipsheds and drydock-like maintenance infrastructure do not depend on merchant partnership contracts.
